---
id: "13.1.8"
title: "Splunk Deployment Server Health, Serverclass Distribution Coverage, Forwarder Phone-Home Latency, and Configuration Drift Detection"
criticality: "critical"
splunkPillar: "Platform"
---

# UC-13.1.8 · Splunk Deployment Server Health, Serverclass Distribution Coverage, Forwarder Phone-Home Latency, and Configuration Drift Detection

## Description

Detects Splunk deployment server management-plane failures by correlating deployment server DeploymentServer splunkd errors, forwarder DeploymentClient connection failures, phonehome_connections cadence drift beyond three times phoneHomeIntervalInSecs, REST-polled deployment client rows against inventory and expected serverclass membership, REST serverclass coverage gaps between subscribed and installed counts, checksum drift when forwarder-side lastChange diverges from deployment server repository_checksum for the same app, and download_state or installState anomalies that imply stale bundles while tcp paths may still appear healthy.

## Value

Platform and compliance stakeholders receive a tamper-resistant ledger that explains why forwarders might ingest with yesterday’s outputs or inputs even when network dashboards look green: deployment server reload faults, class filter mistakes, phone-home starvation, REST-visible membership skew, and checksum drift become one searchable narrative tied to owners and environments so ingestion gaps are fixed at the configuration source rather than misdiagnosed as generic connectivity noise.

## Implementation

Schedule platform_uc_13_1_8_deployment_server_governance every ten minutes with earliest=-6h@h from a platform search head, keep forwarder_fleet_inventory.csv and serverclass_membership_expectations.csv aligned to CMDB and serverclass.conf intent, index REST deployment clients and serverclasses into splunk_platform on a five- to fifteen-minute cadence, tune coverage_gap_pct and phone-home multipliers per environment class, route page rows to deployment server on-call with MC drilldowns, and archive weekly alert exports for compliance evidence.

## Detailed Implementation

Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the Splunk platform engineering team that operates deployment servers, deployment server clusters behind load balancers, universal forwarder and heavy forwarder fleets, and the Monitoring Console tier that analysts use for forwarder management views. This use case isolates the deployment management plane: the deployment server publishes serverclass membership, application bundles, and repository checksums that forwarders reconcile through deploymentclient, while forwarders phone home on a cadence governed by phoneHomeIntervalInSecs and expose lastPhoneHomeTime semantics you can compare against wall-clock drift. When that plane fails, forwarders continue running cached serverclass.xml and app directories that may omit new inputs, new outputs, or corrected index routing, producing silent ingestion gaps that UC-13.1.3’s TCP connectivity story will not explain because bytes may still flow to yesterday’s indexer list. UC-13.1.3 remains authoritative for tcpout queue pressure, auto load-balanced receiver churn, and generic phonehome_connections socket health; this document answers whether the deployment server actually published coherent bundles, whether REST-visible client rows match inventory expectations for each serverClass and app, whether lastChange on the forwarder matches repository_checksum published for the same app, and whether splunkd on the deployment server is throwing reload or state-machine errors that stall distribution.

Architecture context matters before you tune thresholds. A single deployment server can manage tens of thousands of deployment clients when CPU, storage, and phone-home concurrency limits are respected; many estates shard fleets across multiple deployment servers using DNS names, load balancers, or region-specific URIs in deploymentclient.conf. Each serverclass stanza in serverclass.conf filters clients into application bundles; accidental whitelist drift, DNS typos, or overlapping filters can subscribe the wrong hosts while dashboards still show green TCP paths. Repository checksums advance when administrators change files under $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/deployment-apps; clients record lastChange locally when they materialize those changes into $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps. Comparing those two worlds is the heart of configuration drift detection, not generic host uptime.

Data arrives from five complementary lanes Splunk multisearch can fuse without pretending a single sourcetype tells the whole story. Lane one is index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=DeploymentServer on the deployment server hosts, capturing ERROR and WARN lines plus high-signal reload and checksum narratives. Lane two is index=_internal splunkd.log or sourcetype=splunkd component=DeploymentClient on forwarders, capturing unreachable deployment server, TLS, and DNS failures that mean the client never applied the latest bundle. Lane three is metrics.log group=phonehome_connections on forwarders, grounding drift math in sampled phone-home cadence rather than only prose errors. Lane four is your indexed representation of REST GET /services/deployment/server/clients with fields such as clientName, hostname, clientIP, lastPhoneHomeTime, phoneHomeIntervalInSecs, serverClass, app, appNames, installState, and download_state as your collectors expose them. Lane five is indexed GET /services/deployment/server/serverclasses rows with repository_checksum and subscription counts you normalize into subscribedCount versus installedCount style coverage metrics.

RBAC and privacy: REST collectors must authenticate with least-privilege tokens or service accounts that can read deployment endpoints without blanket admin rights on unrelated objects. _internal and REST exports reveal hostnames, IP addresses, and sometimes paths; restrict roles that can export wide time windows, and stamp change_ticket_id onto summary indexes when maintenance explains benign reload storms.

Risk briefing: every large rollout produces transient reload activity; measure dwell time and fleet fraction before paging application teams. Load-balanced deployment servers can skew phone-home toward one member during uneven health checks; pair Splunk rows with balancer and deployment server access logs before blaming forwarders. Clock skew between forwarders and search heads distorts lastPhoneHomeTime comparisons unless you normalize with indexer receipt time and NTP audits.

Differentiation recap: deployment server distribution correctness, serverclass coverage math, phone-home interval drift against phoneHomeIntervalInSecs, checksum and lastChange drift, and deployment server splunkd errors—not generic forwarder-to-indexer TCP health as the primary story, not search head clustering, not KV store replication, not indexer queue saturation.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five ingestion lanes plus two lookups Splunk joins for expected membership context.

Lane A, deployment server splunkd: forward or locally index DeploymentServer component lines from every deployment server host into index=_internal with sourcetype=splunkd. Preserve full _raw for reload and checksum exceptions; avoid transforms that strip stack traces during the first ninety days after a platform upgrade.

Lane B, deployment client splunkd: ensure universal forwarders and heavy forwarders emit DeploymentClient ERROR and WARN lines to the analytics tier. Universal forwarders in hardened estates sometimes route _internal away; maintain a dedicated summary forwarder or HTTP Event Collector path for high-severity deploymentclient strings when full _internal fan-in is politically impossible.

Lane C, phonehome_connections metrics: confirm metrics.log reaches index=_internal with group=phonehome_connections and fields that include phoneHomeIntervalInSecs or equivalent periodicity hints your build exposes. Align collection interval with monitoring search earliest window so drift math does not compare sparse samples to aggressive thresholds.

Lane D, REST clients poll: schedule a hardened collector every five to fifteen minutes to call https://your-ds:8089/services/deployment/server/clients with output_mode=json or csv, normalize clientName, hostname, clientIP, lastPhoneHomeTime, phoneHomeIntervalInSecs, serverClass, app, appNames, installState, and download_state, and index into index=splunk_platform with sourcetype=deployment_rest_clients. Include splunk_server or ds_cluster tags so multisite estates can split alerts.

Lane E, REST serverclasses poll: on the same cadence, call /services/deployment/server/serverclasses, flatten repository_checksum, application lists, and subscription counters into subscribedCount and installedCount style fields, and index with sourcetype=deployment_rest_serverclasses. Store both successful and throttled responses so you can distinguish empty REST results from collector failure.

Lookup F, forwarder_fleet_inventory.csv: maintain hostname keyed rows with env, owner_team, ds_cluster, and tier_tag at minimum. Host keys must match the lowercase hostname normalization used in SPL. Refresh nightly during change seasons.

Lookup G, serverclass_membership_expectations.csv: maintain hostname, serverClass, expected_min_fraction, and baseline_app_family columns describing which hosts should receive which bundles for compliance tagging. This is the subsearch join that answers expected versus observed membership without hard-coding filters inside SPL.

props.conf guidance: TRUNCATE generously for REST JSON, TIMESTAMP_FIELDS aligned to collector _time, and FIELDALIAS maps that preserve both camelCase REST names and snake_case convenience fields.

Pre-save validation: index=_internal component=DeploymentServer earliest=-1h must return rows after deployment server restart drills; deployment_rest_clients must list every lab forwarder you expect; phonehome_connections must move when you force a forwarder reload under vendor guidance.

Dashboards: Monitoring Console Forwarder Management views complement this UC; embed links to deployment server class filters and REST-backed tables for executive briefings.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_1_8_deployment_server_governance with a ten-minute schedule in production, dispatch earliest=-6h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is page or warn after baselining. Throttle duplicate hostname and serverClass rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action text in pager templates so operators open deploymentclient.conf, serverclass.conf, and REST poll logs without improvising.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans deployment server splunkd, deployment client splunkd, phonehome metrics, REST clients, and REST serverclasses arms so a silent single lane cannot hide a genuine distribution failure. coalesce() ladders absorb hostname, clientName, checksum, and installState naming drift across collector versions. streamstats sums ph_breach across recent samples per hostname to catch sticky phone-home latency. eventstats aggregates subscribed and installed hints per serverClass for coverage_gap_pct math from REST clients. join brings forwarder_fleet_inventory.csv for ownership and environment, join matches serverclass_membership_expectations.csv for compliance context, and join enriches change_audit_nearby counts from accelerated Splunk CIM Change summaries via tstats for audit-adjacent bundle mutations. case() maps deployment server errors, deployment client faults, checksum drift, coverage gaps, and phone-home breaches into page versus warn versus info tiers.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.8 Deployment server health, serverclass coverage, phone-home drift, checksum drift, DS state. Tunables earliest=-6h@h ph_mult=3 cov_warn_pct=8 REST index=splunk_platform")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal earliest=-6h@h latest=now sourcetype=splunkd component=DeploymentServer (log_level=ERROR OR log_level=WARN OR match(_raw,"(?i)reload|state|checksum|exception"))
      | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval lane="ds_splunkd"
      | eval clientName=coalesce(clientName, hostname, "ds_host")
      | eval clientIP=coalesce(clientIP, "127.0.0.1")
      | eval serverClass=""
      | eval app=""
      | eval appNames=""
      | eval lastChange=""
      | eval installState=coalesce(log_level, "unknown")
      | eval repository_checksum=coalesce(repository_checksum, "none")
      | eval download_state="unset"
      | eval lastPhoneHomeTime=null()
      | eval phoneHomeIntervalInSecs=0
      | eval subscribedCount=0
      | eval installedCount=0
      | eval depcli_fault=0
      | fields _time hostname lane clientName clientIP serverClass app appNames lastChange installState repository_checksum download_state lastPhoneHomeTime phoneHomeIntervalInSecs subscribedCount installedCount depcli_fault log_level ]
    [ search index=_internal earliest=-6h@h latest=now (sourcetype=splunkd OR source=*splunkd.log*) component=DeploymentClient (log_level=ERROR OR log_level=WARN OR match(_raw,"(?i)unable|fail|timeout|connection"))
      | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval lane="depcli_splunkd"
      | eval clientName=coalesce(clientName, hostname, "forwarder")
      | eval clientIP=coalesce(clientIP, "0.0.0.0")
      | eval serverClass=""
      | eval app=""
      | eval appNames=""
      | eval lastChange=""
      | eval installState="deploymentclient_error"
      | eval repository_checksum="none"
      | eval download_state="error_path"
      | eval lastPhoneHomeTime=null()
      | eval phoneHomeIntervalInSecs=0
      | eval subscribedCount=0
      | eval installedCount=0
      | eval depcli_fault=1
      | fields _time hostname lane clientName clientIP serverClass app appNames lastChange installState repository_checksum download_state lastPhoneHomeTime phoneHomeIntervalInSecs subscribedCount installedCount depcli_fault log_level ]
    [ search index=_internal earliest=-6h@h latest=now sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log group=phonehome_connections
      | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval lane="phonehome_metrics"
      | eval clientName=coalesce(clientName, hostname, "uf_client")
      | eval clientIP=coalesce(clientIP, "0.0.0.0")
      | eval serverClass=coalesce(serverClass, "")
      | eval app=""
      | eval appNames=coalesce(appNames, "")
      | eval lastChange=""
      | eval installState="phonehome_ok"
      | eval repository_checksum="none"
      | eval download_state=coalesce(download_state, "idle")
      | eval lastPhoneHomeTime=_time
      | eval phoneHomeIntervalInSecs=tonumber(coalesce(phoneHomeIntervalInSecs, phoneHomeInterval, 300))
      | eval subscribedCount=0
      | eval installedCount=0
      | eval depcli_fault=0
      | fields _time hostname lane clientName clientIP serverClass app appNames lastChange installState repository_checksum download_state lastPhoneHomeTime phoneHomeIntervalInSecs subscribedCount installedCount depcli_fault ]
    [ search index=splunk_platform earliest=-6h@h latest=now sourcetype=deployment_rest_clients
      | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hostname, host, clientName))))
      | eval clientName=coalesce(clientName, hostname, "rest_client")
      | eval clientIP=coalesce(clientIP, clientip, "0.0.0.0")
      | eval serverClass=coalesce(serverClass, serverclass, "")
      | eval app=coalesce(app, "")
      | eval appNames=coalesce(appNames, app_list, "")
      | eval lastChange=coalesce(lastChange, lastchange, "")
      | eval installState=coalesce(installState, installstate, "unknown")
      | eval repository_checksum=coalesce(repository_checksum, repositoryChecksum, "none")
      | eval download_state=coalesce(downloadState, download_state, "unknown")
      | eval lastPhoneHomeTime=coalesce(strptime(lastPhoneHomeTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(lastPhoneHomeTime,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), _time)
      | eval phoneHomeIntervalInSecs=tonumber(coalesce(phoneHomeIntervalInSecs, 300))
      | eval subscribedCount=tonumber(coalesce(subscribedCount, class_subscribed, 0))
      | eval installedCount=tonumber(coalesce(installedCount, class_installed, 0))
      | eval depcli_fault=0
      | eval lane="rest_clients"
      | fields _time hostname lane clientName clientIP serverClass app appNames lastChange installState repository_checksum download_state lastPhoneHomeTime phoneHomeIntervalInSecs subscribedCount installedCount depcli_fault ]
    [ search index=splunk_platform earliest=-6h@h latest=now sourcetype=deployment_rest_serverclasses
      | eval hostname="deployment_server"
      | eval clientName="serverclass_row"
      | eval clientIP="0.0.0.0"
      | eval serverClass=coalesce(serverClass, name, id, title, "unknown_class")
      | eval app=coalesce(app, appName, "")
      | eval appNames=coalesce(appNames, "")
      | eval lastChange=coalesce(lastChange, "")
      | eval installState=coalesce(state, "serverclass_snapshot")
      | eval repository_checksum=coalesce(repository_checksum, repo_checksum, "none")
      | eval download_state="repository"
      | eval lastPhoneHomeTime=_time
      | eval phoneHomeIntervalInSecs=0
      | eval subscribedCount=tonumber(coalesce(clients_subscribed, 0))
      | eval installedCount=tonumber(coalesce(clients_installed, apps_installed, 0))
      | eval depcli_fault=0
      | eval lane="rest_serverclasses"
      | fields _time hostname lane clientName clientIP serverClass app appNames lastChange installState repository_checksum download_state lastPhoneHomeTime phoneHomeIntervalInSecs subscribedCount installedCount depcli_fault ]
| eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hostname, clientName, "unknown_host"))))
| eval clientName=coalesce(clientName, hostname, "unknown_client")
| eval clientIP=coalesce(clientIP, "0.0.0.0")
| eval serverClass=coalesce(serverClass, "")
| eval app=coalesce(app, "")
| eval appNames=coalesce(appNames, "")
| eval lastChange=coalesce(lastChange, "")
| eval installState=coalesce(installState, "unknown")
| eval repository_checksum=coalesce(repository_checksum, "none")
| eval download_state=coalesce(download_state, "unknown")
| eval phoneHomeIntervalInSecs=tonumber(coalesce(phoneHomeIntervalInSecs, 300))
| eval lastPH_epoch=if(isnum(lastPhoneHomeTime), lastPhoneHomeTime, if(len(toString(lastPhoneHomeTime))>8, strptime(lastPhoneHomeTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), _time))
| eval ph_drift_sec=if(isnotnull(lastPH_epoch), max(0,now()-lastPH_epoch), 0)
| eval ph_breach=if(phoneHomeIntervalInSecs>0 AND ph_drift_sec>(phoneHomeIntervalInSecs*3),1,0)
| sort 0 hostname lane - _time
| streamstats window=4 current=t global=f sum(ph_breach) AS ph_breach_window by hostname
| eventstats sum(eval(if(lane=="rest_clients",subscribedCount,0))) AS sc_sub_sum sum(eval(if(lane=="rest_clients",installedCount,0))) AS sc_ins_sum by serverClass
| eval coverage_gap_pct=if(subscribedCount>0, round(100*max(0,subscribedCount-installedCount)/subscribedCount,3), if(sc_sub_sum>0 AND len(serverClass)>0, round(100*max(0,sc_sub_sum-sc_ins_sum)/sc_sub_sum,3), 0))
| eval checksum_drift=if(len(lastChange)>4 AND len(repository_checksum)>4 AND repository_checksum!="none" AND lastChange!=repository_checksum AND match(installState,"(?i)installed|enabled|complete"),1,0)
| join type=left max=0 hostname
    [| inputlookup forwarder_fleet_inventory.csv
     | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hostname, host, clientName))))
     | fields hostname env owner_team ds_cluster tier_tag ]
| join type=left max=0 hostname serverClass
    [| inputlookup serverclass_membership_expectations.csv
     | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(hostname)))
     | eval serverClass=lower(trim(toString(serverClass)))
     | fields hostname serverClass expected_min_fraction baseline_app_family ]
| join type=left max=0 hostname
    [| tstats summariesonly=true count AS change_audit_nearby FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-24h latest=now BY Change.dest
     | rename Change.dest AS hostname
     | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(hostname))) ]
| eval severity=case(
    lane=="ds_splunkd" AND log_level=="ERROR", "page",
    depcli_fault==1 AND lane=="depcli_splunkd", "page",
    ph_breach_window>=2, "page",
    checksum_drift==1, "warn",
    coverage_gap_pct>=8, "warn",
    ph_breach==1, "warn",
    depcli_fault==1, "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="page" AND lane=="ds_splunkd", "tail_DS_splunkd_verify_reload_cycle_and_serverclass_state_machine_reduce_app_volume",
    severity=="page" AND lane=="depcli_splunkd", "verify_deploymentclient_uri_tls_dns_and_middlebox_timeouts",
    severity=="page" AND ph_breach_window>=2, "scale_DS_phonehome_capacity_validate_lb_health_and_forwarder_flood",
    checksum_drift==1, "compare_forwarder_serverclass_xml_lastChange_to_DS_repository_checksum_redeploy_app",
    coverage_gap_pct>=8, "audit_serverclass_whitelist_and_REST_client_rows_for_missing_installState",
    true(), "monitor_splunk_platform_REST_pollers_and_MC_forwarder_management")
| table _time hostname clientName clientIP serverClass app appNames lastChange installState repository_checksum download_state phoneHomeIntervalInSecs ph_drift_sec coverage_gap_pct checksum_drift lane severity recommended_action env owner_team change_audit_nearby ph_breach_window
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[platform_uc_13_1_8_deployment_server_governance_alert]
cron_schedule = */10 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -6h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_1_8_deployment_server_governance | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch skew, materialize REST polls hourly into a summary index keyed on hostname and serverClass, widen alert earliest to -2h@h on the summary, and retain this full search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the deployment server. Compare Monitoring Console or forwarder management UI client lists to deployment_rest_clients rows for the same window, verifying clientName and hostname keys match after lowercase normalization. Spot-check clientIP for unexpected NAT pools that explain duplicated or missing rows.

On a suspect forwarder, run splunkd cmd btool deploymentclient list --debug and compare targetUri to the DNS name your REST collectors call. Open serverclass.xml under $SPLUNK_HOME/etc and compare lastChange attributes for an app to repository_checksum in the REST serverclass snapshot; mismatches should align with Splunk checksum_drift hints.

Phone-home drift: compare metrics.log phonehome timestamps to lastPhoneHomeTime in REST; large gaps implicate indexing delay or clock skew rather than genuine silence. Validate NTP on forwarders and deployment servers before executive escalation.

Synthetic positive test: in lab, introduce a deliberate checksum mismatch by staging a harmless props.conf tweak under deployment-apps, reload deployment server under change control, and confirm platform_uc_13_1_8_deployment_server_governance surfaces warn-tier checksum or coverage signals within two intervals.

Synthetic negative test: after healthy rollout completion, confirm severity returns to info for hosts not in change windows and that the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to page and warn only.

Job Inspector: watch scan counts on splunk_platform; unexpected explosions suggest unbounded wildcards in REST sourcetypes.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — DeploymentServer ERROR during repository reload: inspect splunkd.log for state-machine exceptions, validate disk latency on $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/deployment-apps, and reduce concurrent app edits before restarting the deployment server under change control.

Case 2 — DeploymentClient cannot resolve deployment server DNS: fix forwarder resolvers, clear stale systemd-resolved caches on Linux, and validate split-horizon DNS for management VLANs.

Case 3 — TLS handshake failures after certificate rotation: align server.pem and caCertFile trust chains on deployment server and forwarders, reload splunkd on both sides, and confirm cipher suites match corporate policy.

Case 4 — Load balancer pinned all phone-home traffic to one deployment server peer: rebalance health checks, verify sticky-session mistakes, and validate connection counts per peer.

Case 5 — serverclass whitelist accidentally excluded a finance subnet: reconcile serverclass.conf filters with CMDB exports, restore membership, and document the filter change in change records.

Case 6 — repository_checksum updated but forwarders show stale lastChange: force a forwarder reload under vendor guidance, verify deployment server class order and cross-class exclusions, and check for full disks blocking app downloads.

Case 7 — download_state stuck while installState claims progress: clear transient network proxies blocking bundle downloads, validate permissions on deployment-apps staging paths, and inspect forwarder-side splunkd.log for download errors.

Case 8 — coverage_gap_pct high for Splunk_TA_nix while TCP ingestion looks healthy: UC-13.1.3 may still be green; focus here on missing installState confirmations and REST subscriber counts rather than tcpout queues.

Case 9 — REST poll empty while UI shows clients: repair collector credentials, fix output_mode parsing, and confirm index=splunk_platform retention did not age out events.

Case 10 — Accelerated Change datamodel missing for tstats join: accelerate CIM Change summaries or downgrade the join arm to a CSV audit feed until acceleration completes.

Case 11 — Phased maintenance with intentional deployment server downtime: annotate forwarder_fleet_inventory tier_tag and suppress page routing only when change_ticket_id and time bounds are explicit in summary indexes.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists deployment server splunkd, deployment client splunkd, phonehome metrics, REST clients, and REST serverclasses arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, join, and tstats appear; final table lists hostname, client identity, serverClass, app metadata, checksum fields, phone-home drift, coverage gap, severity, and ownership columns for analyst drilldowns.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.8 Deployment server health, serverclass coverage, phone-home drift, checksum drift, DS state. Tunables earliest=-6h@h ph_mult=3 cov_warn_pct=8 REST index=splunk_platform")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal earliest=-6h@h latest=now sourcetype=splunkd component=DeploymentServer (log_level=ERROR OR log_level=WARN OR match(_raw,"(?i)reload|state|checksum|exception"))
      | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval lane="ds_splunkd"
      | eval clientName=coalesce(clientName, hostname, "ds_host")
      | eval clientIP=coalesce(clientIP, "127.0.0.1")
      | eval serverClass=""
      | eval app=""
      | eval appNames=""
      | eval lastChange=""
      | eval installState=coalesce(log_level, "unknown")
      | eval repository_checksum=coalesce(repository_checksum, "none")
      | eval download_state="unset"
      | eval lastPhoneHomeTime=null()
      | eval phoneHomeIntervalInSecs=0
      | eval subscribedCount=0
      | eval installedCount=0
      | eval depcli_fault=0
      | fields _time hostname lane clientName clientIP serverClass app appNames lastChange installState repository_checksum download_state lastPhoneHomeTime phoneHomeIntervalInSecs subscribedCount installedCount depcli_fault log_level ]
    [ search index=_internal earliest=-6h@h latest=now (sourcetype=splunkd OR source=*splunkd.log*) component=DeploymentClient (log_level=ERROR OR log_level=WARN OR match(_raw,"(?i)unable|fail|timeout|connection"))
      | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval lane="depcli_splunkd"
      | eval clientName=coalesce(clientName, hostname, "forwarder")
      | eval clientIP=coalesce(clientIP, "0.0.0.0")
      | eval serverClass=""
      | eval app=""
      | eval appNames=""
      | eval lastChange=""
      | eval installState="deploymentclient_error"
      | eval repository_checksum="none"
      | eval download_state="error_path"
      | eval lastPhoneHomeTime=null()
      | eval phoneHomeIntervalInSecs=0
      | eval subscribedCount=0
      | eval installedCount=0
      | eval depcli_fault=1
      | fields _time hostname lane clientName clientIP serverClass app appNames lastChange installState repository_checksum download_state lastPhoneHomeTime phoneHomeIntervalInSecs subscribedCount installedCount depcli_fault log_level ]
    [ search index=_internal earliest=-6h@h latest=now sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log group=phonehome_connections
      | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host, hostname, splunk_server))))
      | eval lane="phonehome_metrics"
      | eval clientName=coalesce(clientName, hostname, "uf_client")
      | eval clientIP=coalesce(clientIP, "0.0.0.0")
      | eval serverClass=coalesce(serverClass, "")
      | eval app=""
      | eval appNames=coalesce(appNames, "")
      | eval lastChange=""
      | eval installState="phonehome_ok"
      | eval repository_checksum="none"
      | eval download_state=coalesce(download_state, "idle")
      | eval lastPhoneHomeTime=_time
      | eval phoneHomeIntervalInSecs=tonumber(coalesce(phoneHomeIntervalInSecs, phoneHomeInterval, 300))
      | eval subscribedCount=0
      | eval installedCount=0
      | eval depcli_fault=0
      | fields _time hostname lane clientName clientIP serverClass app appNames lastChange installState repository_checksum download_state lastPhoneHomeTime phoneHomeIntervalInSecs subscribedCount installedCount depcli_fault ]
    [ search index=splunk_platform earliest=-6h@h latest=now sourcetype=deployment_rest_clients
      | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hostname, host, clientName))))
      | eval clientName=coalesce(clientName, hostname, "rest_client")
      | eval clientIP=coalesce(clientIP, clientip, "0.0.0.0")
      | eval serverClass=coalesce(serverClass, serverclass, "")
      | eval app=coalesce(app, "")
      | eval appNames=coalesce(appNames, app_list, "")
      | eval lastChange=coalesce(lastChange, lastchange, "")
      | eval installState=coalesce(installState, installstate, "unknown")
      | eval repository_checksum=coalesce(repository_checksum, repositoryChecksum, "none")
      | eval download_state=coalesce(downloadState, download_state, "unknown")
      | eval lastPhoneHomeTime=coalesce(strptime(lastPhoneHomeTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), strptime(lastPhoneHomeTime,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), _time)
      | eval phoneHomeIntervalInSecs=tonumber(coalesce(phoneHomeIntervalInSecs, 300))
      | eval subscribedCount=tonumber(coalesce(subscribedCount, class_subscribed, 0))
      | eval installedCount=tonumber(coalesce(installedCount, class_installed, 0))
      | eval depcli_fault=0
      | eval lane="rest_clients"
      | fields _time hostname lane clientName clientIP serverClass app appNames lastChange installState repository_checksum download_state lastPhoneHomeTime phoneHomeIntervalInSecs subscribedCount installedCount depcli_fault ]
    [ search index=splunk_platform earliest=-6h@h latest=now sourcetype=deployment_rest_serverclasses
      | eval hostname="deployment_server"
      | eval clientName="serverclass_row"
      | eval clientIP="0.0.0.0"
      | eval serverClass=coalesce(serverClass, name, id, title, "unknown_class")
      | eval app=coalesce(app, appName, "")
      | eval appNames=coalesce(appNames, "")
      | eval lastChange=coalesce(lastChange, "")
      | eval installState=coalesce(state, "serverclass_snapshot")
      | eval repository_checksum=coalesce(repository_checksum, repo_checksum, "none")
      | eval download_state="repository"
      | eval lastPhoneHomeTime=_time
      | eval phoneHomeIntervalInSecs=0
      | eval subscribedCount=tonumber(coalesce(clients_subscribed, 0))
      | eval installedCount=tonumber(coalesce(clients_installed, apps_installed, 0))
      | eval depcli_fault=0
      | eval lane="rest_serverclasses"
      | fields _time hostname lane clientName clientIP serverClass app appNames lastChange installState repository_checksum download_state lastPhoneHomeTime phoneHomeIntervalInSecs subscribedCount installedCount depcli_fault ]
| eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hostname, clientName, "unknown_host"))))
| eval clientName=coalesce(clientName, hostname, "unknown_client")
| eval clientIP=coalesce(clientIP, "0.0.0.0")
| eval serverClass=coalesce(serverClass, "")
| eval app=coalesce(app, "")
| eval appNames=coalesce(appNames, "")
| eval lastChange=coalesce(lastChange, "")
| eval installState=coalesce(installState, "unknown")
| eval repository_checksum=coalesce(repository_checksum, "none")
| eval download_state=coalesce(download_state, "unknown")
| eval phoneHomeIntervalInSecs=tonumber(coalesce(phoneHomeIntervalInSecs, 300))
| eval lastPH_epoch=if(isnum(lastPhoneHomeTime), lastPhoneHomeTime, if(len(toString(lastPhoneHomeTime))>8, strptime(lastPhoneHomeTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), _time))
| eval ph_drift_sec=if(isnotnull(lastPH_epoch), max(0,now()-lastPH_epoch), 0)
| eval ph_breach=if(phoneHomeIntervalInSecs>0 AND ph_drift_sec>(phoneHomeIntervalInSecs*3),1,0)
| sort 0 hostname lane - _time
| streamstats window=4 current=t global=f sum(ph_breach) AS ph_breach_window by hostname
| eventstats sum(eval(if(lane=="rest_clients",subscribedCount,0))) AS sc_sub_sum sum(eval(if(lane=="rest_clients",installedCount,0))) AS sc_ins_sum by serverClass
| eval coverage_gap_pct=if(subscribedCount>0, round(100*max(0,subscribedCount-installedCount)/subscribedCount,3), if(sc_sub_sum>0 AND len(serverClass)>0, round(100*max(0,sc_sub_sum-sc_ins_sum)/sc_sub_sum,3), 0))
| eval checksum_drift=if(len(lastChange)>4 AND len(repository_checksum)>4 AND repository_checksum!="none" AND lastChange!=repository_checksum AND match(installState,"(?i)installed|enabled|complete"),1,0)
| join type=left max=0 hostname
    [| inputlookup forwarder_fleet_inventory.csv
     | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hostname, host, clientName))))
     | fields hostname env owner_team ds_cluster tier_tag ]
| join type=left max=0 hostname serverClass
    [| inputlookup serverclass_membership_expectations.csv
     | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(hostname)))
     | eval serverClass=lower(trim(toString(serverClass)))
     | fields hostname serverClass expected_min_fraction baseline_app_family ]
| join type=left max=0 hostname
    [| tstats summariesonly=true count AS change_audit_nearby FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-24h latest=now BY Change.dest
     | rename Change.dest AS hostname
     | eval hostname=lower(trim(toString(hostname))) ]
| eval severity=case(
    lane=="ds_splunkd" AND log_level=="ERROR", "page",
    depcli_fault==1 AND lane=="depcli_splunkd", "page",
    ph_breach_window>=2, "page",
    checksum_drift==1, "warn",
    coverage_gap_pct>=8, "warn",
    ph_breach==1, "warn",
    depcli_fault==1, "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="page" AND lane=="ds_splunkd", "tail_DS_splunkd_verify_reload_cycle_and_serverclass_state_machine_reduce_app_volume",
    severity=="page" AND lane=="depcli_splunkd", "verify_deploymentclient_uri_tls_dns_and_middlebox_timeouts",
    severity=="page" AND ph_breach_window>=2, "scale_DS_phonehome_capacity_validate_lb_health_and_forwarder_flood",
    checksum_drift==1, "compare_forwarder_serverclass_xml_lastChange_to_DS_repository_checksum_redeploy_app",
    coverage_gap_pct>=8, "audit_serverclass_whitelist_and_REST_client_rows_for_missing_installState",
    true(), "monitor_splunk_platform_REST_pollers_and_MC_forwarder_management")
| table _time hostname clientName clientIP serverClass app appNames lastChange installState repository_checksum download_state phoneHomeIntervalInSecs ph_drift_sec coverage_gap_pct checksum_drift lane severity recommended_action env owner_team change_audit_nearby ph_breach_window
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Change.action) AS change_action latest(Change.object) AS change_object count FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Change.dest Change.user
| rename Change.dest AS chg_host Change.user AS change_user
| eval chg_host=lower(trim(toString(chg_host)))
| where like(lower(change_object), "%deployment%") OR like(lower(change_action), "%deploy%")
| table chg_host change_user change_action change_object
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by hostname and serverClass with drilldowns to DeploymentServer and DeploymentClient splunkd rows, phonehome metrics, REST client JSON, serverclass snapshots, inventory fields, and Change model context; single-value tiles for coverage_gap_pct and ph_breach_window.
